item.title: Temasek-Backed Manipal Hospitals Files for $1 Billion India IPO
item.description: Manipal Health Enterprises Pvt. has filed draft papers for an initial public offering, setting the stage for what could be India’s largest listing by a hospital operator.

item.title: Futures, Pound Fall on Iran War Caution
UBS Global Cuts India, Euro Zone Stocks on High Oil Sensitivity
item.description: UBS Global Wealth Management downgraded Indian and euro zone equities, warning their sensitivity to elevated oil prices makes them more vulnerable if the Middle East conflict drags on.

item.title: ECB Must Be Vigilant in Face of Stagflation Risks, Vujcic Says
item.description: The European Central Bank must be “very agile and vigilant” to keep prices in check as the Iran war brings stagflation risks closer, Governing Council member Boris Vujcic said.

item.title: Fuel Crunch From War Threatens South African Wheat, Corn Crops
item.description: Farmers in South Africa are heading into the winter planting season with surging diesel prices and tightening supplies — triggered by the Middle East conflict — threatening production in sub-Saharan Africa’s largest commercial wheat-growing industry.

item.title: Marcos Won’t Spend All Reserves on Peso, Sees 6% Growth by 2028
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. signaled that his government will tolerate weakness in the peso, saying there is a limit to their defense of the currency as market forces drive up the dollar.

item.title: India’s Economic Activity Slows in March on Iran War, PMIs Show
item.description: India’s economic activity slowed in March, with manufacturing slumping to its lowest in nearly 4.5 years, as factories curtailed output due to gas shortages triggered by the war in Iran, a flash survey by HSBC Holdings Plc showed.

item.title: Oil Crunch Threatens South Korea’s Garbage Bag, Ramen Supply
item.description: Deepening shortages of Middle Eastern crude oil are threatening to disrupt everyday life in South Korea, from trash bags to instant noodles.

item.title: SK Hynix Eyes Up to $10 Billion From Potential US Listing, Daily Says
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. is seeking to raise 10 trillion won to 15 trillion won ($10 billion) from a potential listing in the US, the Korea Economic Daily reported, as companies rush to increase capacity to meet memory chip demand fueled by the artificial-intelligence boom.

item.title: India’s BPCL Taps Trade Director Heda to Lead New Singapore Unit
item.description: Bharat Petroleum Corp. Ltd. will start operating a Singapore office in the quarter beginning April, with an initial focus on procuring crude oil in the trading hub, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oil Market Saw Spike in Trades Ahead of Trump’s Iran Pivot Post
item.description: Contracts representing millions of barrels of oil changed hands about 15 minutes before a social media post from US President Donald Trump that sent crude prices tumbling by as much as 14%.

item.title: Hungary to Hold Rates as Iran Turmoil Upends Monetary Outlook
item.description: Hungary’s central bank is expected to hold its key interest rate at the last policy meeting before elections in April as the turmoil in financial markets has left the country’s assets among the most exposed globally.

item.title: Bank of Finland Cuts Forecast as Energy Shock Lifts Prices
item.description: Finland’s nascent economic recovery is at risk as rising energy prices driven by the war in Iran weigh on growth and add to inflation pressures, the Bank of Finland forecast.

item.title: Japan Preparing Stopgap Budget as Takaichi Gets Reality Check
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is encountering the limits of her historic mandate as she struggles to get an annual budget through parliament in time for the start of the new fiscal year in April despite setting that goal.

item.title: Mixue Reports 33% Jump in Profit Amid Bruising China Price War
item.description: Mixue Group reported more than 30% jump in revenue and profit, in line with analysts estimates, a sign the Chinese beverage chain known for its one-dollar lemonade and milk teas has managed to stick through the bruising consumer price war in 2025.

item.title: Bangladesh Faces Slow Nuclear Start As Energy Crisis Deepens
item.description: Bangladesh will likely commission its first nuclear reactor within months, a long-delayed start that comes as the government grapples with acute energy shortages caused by war and trade disruption in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Singapore to Tighten Policy Over Inflation Risks, Economists Say
item.description: Singapore will likely tighten monetary policy next month and consider further moves later in the year as the Middle East crisis drives up prices in the trade-reliant city-state, according to economists.

item.title: Japan’s 40-Year Bond Sale Demand In Line With 12-Month Average
item.description: Japan’s 40-year government bond auction drew demand that was in line with its 12-month average as higher yields attracted investors despite escalating tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Thailand’s Imports Surge on Gold Shipments, Outpace Exports
item.description: Thailand’s inbound shipments soared while exports growth slowed, even before the conflict in the Middle East began disrupting global trade and raising costs.

item.title: Nintendo Cuts Switch 2 Output by Over 30% on Weak US Sales
item.description: Nintendo Co. is cutting back the production of Switch 2 after demand for the $450 gaming console trailed the company’s expectations during the year-end holiday season, particularly in the US.

item.title: Copper Resumes Losses as Iran War Continues to Batter Metals
item.description: Copper resumed losses after a brief bounce in the previous session, as global inflation and growth concerns due to the Middle East war weighed on the metals complex.

item.title: Asian Corporate Bonds Rebound After Trump Delays Iran Strike
item.description: Asian corporate bond markets rebounded alongside other regional assets, lifted by signs of easing tensions and President Donald Trump’s comments on talks with Iran.

item.title: India Stock Traders Eye Rebound as Trump Delays Energy Strikes on Iran
item.description: Nifty 50 slid to its lowest since early April on Monday.

item.title: Australia to Plant Less Wheat as Global Fertilizer Woes Deepen
item.description: Wheat farmers in Australia — one of the world’s biggest agricultural exporters — are paring back plantings as concerns over fertilizer supplies mount, the latest sign of how the war in Iran is disrupting operations on farms around the world.

item.title: Alibaba Unveils New Chip Design to Meet Surging Demand for AI
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. is launching a new chip for agentic AI and inference computing, adding to a portfolio of semiconductors designed to drive its artificial intelligence ambitions.

item.title: Valero Port Arthur Refinery Has Fire at Diesel Hydrotreater Unit
item.description: Valero Energy Corp. is responding to a fire in a unit at its Port Arthur refinery in Texas, the company said late Monday.

item.title: Japan Is Said to Sound Out Market on Oil Futures Intervention
item.description: Japan’s Finance Ministry is said to have made inquiries with market participants on possible intervention in the crude oil futures market, in an apparent attempt to ease pressure on the commodity and currency.

item.title: Australian Miners Jump Most in a Year on Trump’s Iran Remarks
item.description: Australian mining stocks gained the most in almost a year after President Donald Trump’s decision to postpone attacks on Iran’s energy infrastructure.

item.title: Vietnam, Russia Sign Nuclear Power Deal Amid Energy Pressure
item.description: Vietnam, Russia signed an agreement on the construction of the Southeast Asian nation’s first nuclear power plant as Hanoi moves to bolster energy security amid Middle East disruptions that are disrupting global fuel supplies.

item.title: Chile Hikes Fuel Prices By Up to 54% to Reflect Global Oil Shock
item.description: Chile’s fuel prices will soar this week after the government announced on Monday night that it will loosen a stabilization mechanism to reflect the international oil shock, Finance Minister Jorge Quiroz said.
